description:
You are an integration developer who has just received two raw API log-files
    exported from a staging environment.  Each line in the files starts with an
    ISO-8601 time-stamp, then the literal string “ - ” (space, hyphen, space),
    followed by a single-line JSON object.

    1.  Location of the raw material
        • The logs are already present in
          /home/user/api_logs/raw/
          and have names that start with “service_” and end with “.log”.

    2.  Goal
        From every “*.log” file inside /home/user/api_logs/raw/ you must,
        using ONLY classic text-processing tools that ship with every Linux
        distribution (awk, sed, grep, sort, uniq, cat, etc. — no python, jq,
        perl, node, etc.), produce a concise statistical summary and an error
        catalogue.

    3.  Required output artefacts
        Create the directory
        /home/user/api_logs/summary
        if it does not yet exist, then create exactly the two files below
        inside that directory.

        3.1  /home/user/api_logs/summary/service_status_report.csv
             • CSV, UTF-8, Unix LF line endings.
             • First row is the literal header:
                   service,status,count
             • Every subsequent row represents the total number of
               occurrences of a particular HTTP status code (currently only
               200 and 500 appear) for one service across *all* raw log files.
             • Column rules
                   Col-1 (service) : service field from the JSON
                   Col-2 (status)  : numeric status value
                   Col-3 (count)   : integer occurrences
             • Sorting rules
                   – Rows are ordered alphabetically by service name A→Z.
                   – Within the same service, rows are ordered by the numeric
                     status code in ascending order (200, 201, …, 500, …).
             • No extra spaces before/after commas.
             • No trailing blank lines after the last row.

        3.2  /home/user/api_logs/summary/unique_errors.txt
             • Plain text, UTF-8, Unix LF.
             • Contains every distinct value of the “error” JSON field that
               appeared in any line whose status equals 500.
             • One error string per line, no duplicates.
             • Lines must be sorted alphabetically (A→Z).
             • No leading or trailing spaces on any line and no blank line at
               the end of the file.

    4.  Verification notes
        The automated test will:
        • Check that both files exist at the exact paths given above.
        • Verify the exact byte-for-byte contents of each file.
        • Confirm that no carriage-return characters (\r) are present.
        • Verify that the header of the CSV is spelled exactly as specified.

    5.  Implementation constraints
        • Do *not* install additional programs or use any languages other than
          the classic Unix text tools mentioned.
        • Everything must be done from the regular user account (no sudo).
        • You may assume the raw directory already exists and is readable.
        • Create the summary directory only if it does not already exist.
        • The filenames and paths are case-sensitive; follow them precisely.

    6.  Suggested strategy (informative, not prescriptive)
        • Start by concatenating or looping through the two raw files.
        • Use sed or awk to isolate the JSON fragment after “ - ”.
        • Use awk to extract the “service”, “status”, and, when present,
          “error” fields.
        • Aggregate counts (service+status) with awk’s associative arrays or
          pipeline the output to sort | uniq -c.
        • Output the aggregated counts in the strict CSV order and sorting
          required.
        • Collect the distinct “error” values from status 500 lines, sort, and
          write to unique_errors.txt.

    When both files are in place and perfectly formatted you are finished.
truth:
<!--
        Initial fixture files that are already present when the task starts
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/api_logs/raw/service_2023-05-12.log"><![CDATA[
2023-05-12T14:23:11Z - {"service":"user","endpoint":"/v1/register","status":200,"response_time_ms":132}
2023-05-12T14:25:46Z - {"service":"auth","endpoint":"/v1/login","status":500,"error":"DB_TIMEOUT","response_time_ms":987}
2023-05-12T14:28:09Z - {"service":"user","endpoint":"/v1/profile","status":200,"response_time_ms":84}
2023-05-12T14:31:55Z - {"service":"payment","endpoint":"/v1/pay","status":500,"error":"CARD_DECLINED","response_time_ms":453}
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/api_logs/raw/service_2023-05-13.log"><![CDATA[
2023-05-13T07:12:44Z - {"service":"payment","endpoint":"/v1/refund","status":200,"response_time_ms":211}
2023-05-13T07:15:02Z - {"service":"auth","endpoint":"/v1/login","status":200,"response_time_ms":153}
2023-05-13T07:18:27Z - {"service":"user","endpoint":"/v1/delete","status":500,"error":"UNKNOWN_USER","response_time_ms":674}
2023-05-13T07:20:19Z - {"service":"payment","endpoint":"/v1/pay","status":500,"error":"INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS","response_time_ms":502}
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
        Expected artefacts after successful completion
    -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/api_logs/summary/service_status_report.csv"><![CDATA[
service,status,count
auth,200,1
auth,500,1
payment,200,1
payment,500,2
user,200,2
user,500,1
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/api_logs/summary/unique_errors.txt"><![CDATA[
CARD_DECLINED
DB_TIMEOUT
INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS
UNKNOWN_USER
]]></file>
    </expected_files>
